classification.domain: cybersecurity workforce development and risk management competency

topic.what: Structural causes of cybersecurity risk management competency failure despite professional codification and technical investment
topic.how: Sequential mixed-methods: NLP classification of NIST NICE framework (2,111 TKS statements); SEM (n=126); control group comparison (n=133); 7 senior leadership interviews
topic.why_matters: Cybersecurity speaks risk language but structurally thinks in threats; 60% of SMBs close within 6 months of breach; effective remediation requires fundamental redesign of professional formation not marginal curriculum reform

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of NIST NICE framework TKS statements classified as risk management competency
characteristics.evidence: NLP high-confidence classification of 2,111 TKS statements
characteristics.context: Risk management ranks below 18 higher-priority competency domains in federal workforce framework

characteristics.what_itasures: Presence of likelihood/probability terminology required for formal risk analysis
characteristics.evidence: Zero appearances of 'likelihood' and 'probability' across full NICE corpus
characteristics.context: NIST 800-12 requires risk-commensurate control decisions; this vocabulary is absent from codified workforce competencies

characteristics.what_it_measures: SEM path coefficient for training exposure predicting risk competence
characteristics.context: Training exposure significantly but partially explains competence variance

characteristics.what_it_measures: Combined direct and indirect effects of training exposure on competence
characteristics.evidence: Total effect via conceptual salience (β=.223 indirect); n=126
characteristics.context: Training exposure operates through both direct and mediated pathways

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of cybersecurity professionals achieving high differentiation in foundational risk reasoning
characteristics.context: 88.1% of cybersecurity professionals failed to demonstrate differentiated risk reasoning above general population baseline
